{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Migrated API route handlers from Pages Router pattern (export default function handler(req, res) with NextApiRequest/NextApiResponse) to App Router route handlers (named exports GET, POST, PUT, DELETE with NextRequest/NextResponse). req.query.id replaced by params (a Promise in Next.js 15+, must be awaited). req.body replaced by request.json(). Method-not-allowed (405) responses are handled automatically by Next.js when the handler for a method is not exported — no need for manual Allow header logic.

Why: App Router route handlers use the Web API standard (Request/Response) with NextRequest/NextResponse extensions. Named exports replace the single-handler switch-statement pattern for cleaner separation. Unsupported methods automatically return 405, eliminating boilerplate.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_migration_aecdff67","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["MIG","API","PAG","ROU","NEX","APP","GET","POS","PUT","DEL","PRO","MET","ALL","WHY","WEB","REQ","RES","NAM","UNS"],"field":"general","flags":["DECISION","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":4,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"migration","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":-0.031716108322143555,"source_file":"claude-session: Pages Router to App Router migration","topics":["migrated","api","route","handlers","from","pages","router","pattern","export","default","function","handler","req","res","nextapirequest","nextapiresponse","to","app","named","exports","get","post","put","delete","nextrequest","nextresponse","query","id","replaced","by","params","a","promise","in","next","js","15","must","be","awaited","body","request","json","method","not","allowed","405","responses","are","handled","automatically","when","is","exported","no","need","manual","allow","header","logic","web","standard","response","extensions","replace","single","switch","statement","cleaner","separation","unsupported","methods","return","eliminating","boilerplate"],"wing":"workspace"},{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Split _error.js (Pages Router catch-all error page using getInitialProps to determine statusCode) into two App Router files: not-found.tsx (server component, handles 404 via notFound() calls and invalid routes) and error.tsx (client component with 'use client' directive, receives {error, reset} props for error boundary recovery). Replaced router.isFallback loading pattern with a loading.tsx file in app/blog/[id]/ that Next.js automatically renders during page generation.

Why: App Router separates concerns — not-found.tsx handles 404 errors declaratively, error.tsx handles runtime errors with a reset() retry mechanism. The file-based loading convention (loading.tsx) replaces imperative isFallback checks, providing automatic Suspense boundaries without manual conditional rendering.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_migration_59d952c1","emotions":["anx"],"entities":["SPL","PAG","ROU","APP","REP","NEX","WHY","THE","SUS"],"field":"general","flags":["DECISION","ORIGIN","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":4,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"migration","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":-0.1211780309677124,"source_file":"claude-session: Pages Router to App Router migration","topics":["split","error","js","pages","router","catch","all","page","using","getinitialprops","to","determine","statuscode","into","two","app","files","not","found","tsx","server","component","handles","404","via","notfound","calls","invalid","routes","client","directive","receives","reset","props","boundary","recovery","replaced","isfallback","loading","pattern","a","file","in","blog","id","next","automatically","renders","during","generation","separates","concerns","errors","declaratively","runtime","retry","mechanism","convention","replaces","imper…(+6369)

{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Migrated API route handlers from Pages Router pattern (export default function handler(req, res) with NextApiRequest/NextApiResponse) to App Router route handlers (named exports GET, POST, PUT, DELETE with NextRequest/NextResponse). req.query.id replaced by params (a Promise in Next.js 15+, must be awaited). req.body replaced by request.json(). Method-not-allowed (405) responses are handled automatically by Next.js when the handler for a method is not exported — no need for manual Allow header logic.

Why: App Router route handlers use the Web API standard (Request/Response) with NextRequest/NextResponse extensions. Named exports replace the single-handler switch-statement pattern for cleaner separation. Replaced router.isFallback loading pattern with a loading.tsx file in app/blog/[id]/ that Next.js automatically renders during page generation.

Why: App Router separates concerns — not-found.tsx handles 404 errors declaratively, error.tsx handles runtime errors with a reset() retry mechanism.
